author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
A Bridgehampton, N.Y., resident faces federal charges for lying repeatedly on immigration applications about his leadership role in the horrific Rwandan genocide of 1994.

Our special agents investigated the case and discovered Faustin Nsabumukunzi lied on his green card and citizenship applications. The indictment alleges he led local militant Hutu extremists in his home country when the organized genocide began. It further states he detained victims and participated in local murders.

⚖️A Rwandan court convicted Nsabumukunzi in absentia for genocide.

🔎The Rwandan spree of horror lasted three months, beginning April 1994. An estimated 800K people were slaughtered — many were raped or left permanently maimed. Thousands of Tutsi women were kidnapped and kept as sex slaves.

📃When applying for a green card and U.S. citizenship, Nsabumukunzi denied any involvement as a perpetrator of genocide. He faces one count of visa fraud and two counts of attempted naturalization fraud.

📰Learn more about this case in our newsroom.

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
TULSA, Okla. – The U.S. Attorney’s office charged a Tulsa man for allegedly running an illegal dog fighting camp. We found 17 dogs when we executed the search warrant April 21, with some of the animals missing body parts. One dog was deceased.

Julius Deane Griffin, 43, is charged with:

📌23 counts of Illegal dogfighting — illegal possession, training, transport, delivery, sale and receipt of dogs intended to be used in an animal fighting venture

📌Causing a child to attend an animal fighting venture

📌Felon in possession of ammunition

📌Possession of ammunition after a domestic violence conviction

📌Assault with a dangerous weapon with intent to do great bodily harm

Court records show that he has a previous conviction from 2005 for his involvement in another dogfighting ring involving more than 40 dogs.

The City of Tulsa Animal Services and the Humane Society of Tulsa are tending to the dogs’ medical needs.

📰Read the DOJ press release👇
tweets.text_resolved:

📌Causing a child to attend an animal fighting venture

📌Felon in possession of ammunition

📌Possession of ammunition after a domestic violence conviction

📌Assault with a dangerous weapon with intent to do great bodily harm

Court records show that he has a previous conviction from 2005 for his involvement in another dogfighting ring involving more than 40 dogs.

tweets.text:
COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. —  A monthslong investigation and multiagency federal enforcement action resulted in the arrest of over 100 illegal aliens at an illegal after-hours night club.

The nightclub — guarded by active-duty U.S. servicemembers working as club security — was a hotspot for drug trafficking, violent offenders and prostitution.

The servicemembers are now in the Army CID’s custody, and we have custody of the illegal aliens. Federal law enforcement seized numerous firearms and mounds of illicit drugs.

tweets.text:
BEACON, N.Y.  —  We arrested this Mexican criminal alien April 17, when he was released from serving time for murder and robbery.

The Kings County Supreme Court in Brooklyn sentenced Noel Hernandez Leon to 20-years-to-life in prison back in 1996.

An immigration judge ordered him removed to his home country May 26, 1999 — so he’ll be staying with us until we can execute that removal.

📰Press release: https://t.co/5e8oNeCgdQ
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
HOUGHTON LAKE, Mich. —  We helped arrest this illegal alien April 25. He’s accused of raping an 18-year-old woman. He faces five felony counts of sexual assault.

He was deported in 2014 and expelled under Title 42 in 2023. After that, he reentered the United States illegally again.

📰Learn more about this case in our newsroom.

📌We provide support for people victimized by criminal aliens. If you’re a victim harmed by a criminal alien, contact our VOICE office: 1-855-48-VOICE

tweets.text:
We notified three Colorado businesses of our intent to issue significant fines due to illegal alien employment violations:

🏢 CCS Denver, Inc. 📌Fined $6.1M+ | At least 87 unauthorized workers
🏢 PBC Commercial Cleaning Systems, Inc.📌Fined $1.5M+ | At least 12 unauthorized workers
🏢 Green Management Denver📌Fined $270K+ | 44 unauthorized workers

📋Our worksite compliance tools include I-9 (Employment Eligibility Verification) inspections & civil fines.
🚔Our worksite enforcement options include criminal arrest of employers.

🆘We help businesses comply: https://t.co/IpkWxkNlPm

📰Learn more about this case in our newsroom

💡Learn how law enforcement agencies can partner with us at https://t.co/wh8ZYU9RvR

🔊@CBP provided extraordinary support during this operation. We appreciate our @DHSgov partner's commitment to public safety.

📷Check out the first photos from the field of this highly successful operation.

tweets.text:
ICE Miami arrested documented MS-13 gang member Rafael Juarez-Cabrera in an enforcement operation that took 780+ criminal aliens off the streets in the first four days. He illegally reentered the country three times and has an illegal reentry conviction.

🚨MS-13 is a violent transnational terrorist organization known for trafficking humans, firearms and drugs. They are notorious for violence — including rape, murder, kidnapping and assault.

Juarez-Cabrera will stay in ICE custody pending prosecution and removal proceedings.

This operation is a first-of-its kind partnership between state and federal partners. Learn to partner with us at https://t.co/wh8ZYUaplp

#OperationTidalWave

📞866-DHS-2-ICE

🔎Convicted of homicide in his home country
🔎Sentenced to eight years and eight months in prison
🔎Entered the U.S. illegally at an unknown place and time

This operation is a first-of-its kind partnership between state and federal partners. Learn to partner with us at https://t.co/wh8ZYUaplp

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
ORLANDO, Fla. — A U.S. district judge sentenced three men this week for their roles in a gun trafficking conspiracy.

📌They illegally possessed firearms, ammunition stockpiles, grenades and machine gun conversion devices.

📌Between October and December 2023, they trafficked more than 100 Glock pistols and AK-47 rifles.

Our special agents assisted with the investigation that led to their arrests and convictions.

🔎The case was part of Project Safe Neighborhoods — a program aimed at reducing violent crime & gun violence.

tweets.text:
In a first-of-its-kind partnership between state and federal partners, ICE Miami and Florida law enforcement arrested nearly 800 illegal aliens this week during the first four days of #OperationTidalWave — a massive, multi-agency, immigration enforcement crackdown.

💡Learn how law enforcement agencies can partner with us at https://t.co/wh8ZYU9RvR

🔊@CBP provided extraordinary support during this operation. We appreciate our @DHSgov partner's commitment to public safety.

📷Check out the first photos from the field of this highly successful operation.
